mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is near zero and CPIAUCSL remains contained, the market infers continued monetary accommodation and a lower discount rate path, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not above baseline when FEDFUNDS is at or below 0.25% and CPIAUCSL inflation is stable, or if triggered-period returns are negative on average.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals weakening household income and slower demand, which depresses earnings expectations and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if months with a 0.3pp or larger 3-month rise in UNRATE are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if average returns are not below unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized acceleration in RSAFS and HOUST indicates improving final demand and housing-led cyclical momentum, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if joint improvements in RSAFS and HOUST do not outperform single-series signals, or if 60-day forward SPX returns are not positive in more than half of joint-acceleration episodes.

mechanism_narrative: An improvement in INDPRO with stable or falling CPIAUCSL indicates stronger real output without fresh inflation pressure, easing margin concerns and lifting S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with rising INDPRO and sub-3% CPIAUCSL inflation do not produce positive excess 20-day SPX returns, or if the joint condition loses predictive power after accounting for INDPRO alone.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in GDPC1 relative to prior quarter signals recessionary demand loss that leads investors to cut earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 declines versus the prior quarter
failure_condition: Reject if quarterly GDP contractions are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the average 60-day return is not materially below the unconditional benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained monthly gain in PAYEMS indicates labor-market resilience and supports consumption expectations, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next week when the labor market is still adding jobs.
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive PAYEMS gains do not improve 5-day forward SPX returns relative to baseline, or if the effect disappears when UNRATE is flat or falling.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in FEDFUNDS from prior months is read as policy easing and liquidity support, which should boost equity prices over the next week as discount rates and funding stress fall.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls by at least 50 bps over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if 5-day SPX returns after a 50 bps/2-month FEDFUNDS decline are not higher than unconditional returns, or if the signal flips negative during repeated easing episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS weakens while HOUST also contracts, the combined message is fading consumer and housing demand, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline month over month
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous declines in RSAFS and HOUST are not followed by below-average 20-day SPX returns, or if the joint signal is not stronger than either series alone.
